differences.title: 1. Architecture
differences.leftDesc: Runs as a separate server process, accessed by clients over a network.
differences.rightDesc: Integrated directly into the application, runs in the same process.

differences.title: 2. Concurrency
differences.leftDesc: Handles many simultaneous connections and transactions efficiently.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for single-user or low-concurrency scenarios.

differences.title: 3. Scalability
differences.leftDesc: Scales well for large datasets and high traffic loads.
differences.rightDesc: Best suited for small to medium datasets, not for heavy loads.

differences.title: 4. Data Integrity
differences.leftDesc: Ensures Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability for transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Offers ACID compliance, but with limitations in concurrent writes.

differences.title: 5. Features
differences.leftDesc: Advanced SQL, JSON support, GIS, extensions, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Provides essential SQL features for basic database operations.

differences.title: 6. Management
differences.leftDesc: Needs dedicated administration for setup, maintenance, and security.
differences.rightDesc: No separate server process or complex configuration needed.

differences.title: 7. Use Cases
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for web applications, data warehousing, and complex analytics.
differences.rightDesc: Perfect for mobile apps, desktop applications, and simple data storage.
